英國英格蘭諾福爾可奧比
zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice de Braose於1336年出生在英國諾福爾可時，她的父親Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr30歲，母親Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford26歲。她和Sir William de Cailly至少有1個兒子和7個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice de Braose于1336年出生在英国諾福爾可时，她的父亲Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr30岁，母亲Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford26岁。她和Sir William de Cailly至少有1个儿子和7个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Braose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Eckhard, Gerhard, Manfred, Otto.
from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).

variants.name: Ambrose
meanings: English and Scottish: from the personal name Ambrose (French Ambroise, Latin Ambrosius, from Greek ambrosios ‘immortal, divine’), which was popular throughout Christendom in medieval Europe. Its popularity was due in part to the fame of Saint Ambrose (c. 340–397), one of the four Latin Fathers of the Church, the teacher of Saint Augustine. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Ambrož (see Ambroz ), and also their derivatives, e.g. the Slovenian patronymic Ambrožič.
Irish: from Mac Ambróis ‘son of Ambrose’ (see 1 above); a West Munster name, which has also been Anglicized as McCambridge .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice de Braose was born in 1336, in Norfolk, England, her father, Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr, was 30 and her mother, Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford, was 26. She had at least 1 son and 7 daughters with Sir William de Cailly.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice de Braose est née en 1336, à Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford, avait 26 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 7 filles avec Sir William de Cailly.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice de Braose 1336 in Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr 30 und ihre Mutter Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford 26. Sie hatte mit Sir William de Cailly mindestens 1 Sohn und 7 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alice de Braose è nata il nel 1336 nel Norfolk, England, sua padre, Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford, aveva 26 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 7 figlie con Sir William de Cailly.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice de Braoseが1336年にイギリスノーフォルクで生まれた時，父Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Srは30歳，母Lady Agnes Eva de Uffordは26歳であった。彼女はSir William de Caillyとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と7人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice de Braose(이)가 1336년 영국 노르폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 Sir William de Cailly(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice de Braose nasceu em 1336, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford, tinha 26 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho e 7 filhas com Sir William de Cailly.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice de Braose родилась в 1336 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford, 26. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 7 дочерей от Sir William de Cailly.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alice de Braose nació en 1336, en Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 7 hijas con Sir William de Cailly.
